title: centaurea alba subsp. tenoreana
intro: Centaurea alba subsp. tenoreana, also known as Sardinian knapweed, is a subspecies of the Centaurea alba plant, a perennial herb in the sunflower family native to Sardinia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoid content. The health benefits of Centaurea alba subsp. tenoreana include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of Centaurea alba subsp. tenoreana include tea infusions, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat various health issues, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
